title: Minesweeper
intro: Minesweeper is a logic puzzle played on a grid. Some squares are revealed and show numbers, while others remain unrevealed. Your goal is to identify which unrevealed cells contain mines.
rule_numbered: Each numbered cell tells you how many mines are in its neighboring cells (horizontal, vertical, and diagonal).
example_numbered: In the example below, the cell with a 3 has four neighbors, highlighted in yellow, three of which contain mines.
control_flag_safe: You can flag a cell to mark it as a suspected mine, or place a cross to mark the cell as safe.
example_flags: In the example below, several flags have been placed to mark suspected mines, satisfying the number clues 2, 3, 2, and 1 in the second to last row:
solved: A puzzle is solved when all mines are flagged, the remaining cells are unmarked or marked safe, and all number clues are satisfied (i.e., the number of adjacent flags matches the number on the clue cell).
solved_board: Below is an example of a solved board:
control_cycle: Left click to cycle forward, right click to cycle backward between:
control_place_safe: Placing a safe mark

compact.rule_unrevealed: Unrevealed squares () might hide a mine.
compact.rule_numbers: Numbers indicate how many mines are in the (up to) eight surrounding squares.
compact.rule_flags: Use flags () to mark suspected mines and crosses () to mark squares safe.
